forbiddenClaims: an early-repayment fee, interest-savings figure, or settlement quote fabricated without a live pull
a hardcoded APR, outstanding-balance value, payment-amount value, or settlement figure
a scheduled future payment presented as already-taken, or a taken payment presented as merely scheduled
a downloadable receipt generated for a scheduled-but-not-yet-taken payment
a failed or returned payment hidden behind a generic all-good state
FCA authorisation, Consumer Credit Act compliance certification, or affiliation with any real lender

rubricAnchors.score1: Scheduled and taken payments are indistinguishable, or early repayment fabricates a fee or savings figure.
rubricAnchors.score3: Scheduled and taken payments are tagged but inconsistently, or early repayment shows a plausible-looking but unsourced figure.
rubricAnchors.score5: Scheduled and taken payments are visually and semantically distinct throughout, early repayment surfaces a live settlement quote with no fabricated figure, and failed payments are surfaced honestly.

rubricAnchors.score1: Balance-overview, payment-schedule, and early-repayment share no common scheduled / taken / failed model.
rubricAnchors.score3: Some shared tokens across the three screens but the scheduled-vs-taken tag drifts between overview, schedule, and early repayment.
rubricAnchors.score5: A single direction system unifies all three screens, including a consistent scheduled / taken / failed tag and a consistent settlement-quote pending / ready / expired treatment.

rubricAnchors.score1: No actionable blueprint; engineers cannot infer the payment-due-soon, payment-just-taken, payment-failed-returned, or settlement-quote-expired states.
rubricAnchors.score3: Partial blueprints with unresolved payment-failed-returned or settlement-quote-expired states.
rubricAnchors.score5: Engineers can build all three screens directly, including the four balance-overview states, four payment-schedule states, and five early-repayment states.

rubricAnchors.score1: A direct clone of an existing UK lender's dashboard that fabricates a settlement quote and hides failed payments.
rubricAnchors.score3: Derivative of common loan-dashboard patterns but the scheduled-vs-taken and honest-early-repayment treatments are visually distinguishable.
rubricAnchors.score5: A distinct treatment that makes the scheduled-vs-taken distinction, honest early repayment, and honest failed-payment recovery legible without diluting the dashboard job.

rubricAnchors.score1: No accessibility handling and no payment-failed-returned, balance-load-error, or early-repayment-service-error states.
rubricAnchors.score3: Baseline contrast and focus with partial handling of the failed-payment and balance-load-error states.
rubricAnchors.score5: WCAG-aligned contrast and focus across all three screens, with robust payment-failed-returned, balance-load-error, settlement-quote-expired, and early-repayment-service-error states.

rubricAnchors.score1: A fabricated early-repayment fee, savings figure, settlement quote, APR, or balance value appears anywhere.
rubricAnchors.score3: Most claims trace to the brief but one fee, savings, or settlement figure slips through as a concrete number.
rubricAnchors.score5: Every claim traces to the brief, APR / balance / payment / settlement figures surface as templated placeholders or dynamic pulls, and no FCA or CCA-compliance claim appears.

adjudicationNotes: Any fabricated early-repayment fee, interest-savings figure, or settlement quote is an automatic fail on evidence-discipline, regardless of how polished the dashboard is.
The scheduled-vs-taken distinction is load-bearing across all three screens; conflating them anywhere fails product-appropriateness.
Receipts must be downloadable only for taken payments; allowing a receipt for a scheduled-but-not-taken payment fails evidence-discipline.
Failed or returned payments must be surfaced honestly with a recovery path; hiding a failure behind a generic all-good state fails accessibility-and-failure-states.
Do not prescribe exact copy, brand colours, or pixel-level layout; the brief fixes constraints (distinct states, honest early repayment), not visual identity.
